# RFC 0001 hyphence test vectors.
#
# Each non-comment line: name\tinput-b64\toutcome\texpected-b64
#
# Outcomes are namespaced by the owning harness so multiple test
# harnesses can share this single canonical vector file. An unknown
# outcome inside a harness's own namespace is a typo and fatals;
# outcomes in another harness's namespace are skipped.
#
# Outcomes:
#   legacy/parse-ok                            - DecodeFrom must succeed; blob bytes
#                                                (captured via BlobTeeWriter) must
#                                                equal the base64-decoded expected
#                                                field.
#   legacy/parse-error-missing-separator       - DecodeFrom must error with
#                                                errors.Is(err, errMissingNewline-
#                                                AfterBoundary).
#   document/parse-error-invalid-prefix        - MetadataValidator (via Reader) must
#                                                error with errors.Is(err,
#                                                ErrInvalidPrefix).
#   document/parse-error-malformed-line        - MetadataValidator (via Reader) must
#                                                error with errors.Is(err,
#                                                ErrMalformedMetadataLine).
#   document/parse-error-inline-body-with-at   - Reader must succeed; the post-
#                                                ReadFrom validator state must show
#                                                an @-line was seen AND a body section
#                                                followed (the validate subcommand
#                                                surfaces ErrInlineBodyWithAtReference
#                                                from this state).
#
# Comment lines start with #. Blank lines are ignored.
#
# Plain-text view of each input:
#   minimal-no-body          : "---\n! toml-type-v1\n---\n"
#   body-with-separator      : "---\n! md\n---\n\nhello\n" -> blob "hello\n"
#   body-without-separator   : "---\n! toml-type-v1\n---\nfile-extension = 'png'\n"
#                              (no blank line after closing ---; strict default rejects)

minimal-no-body	LS0tCiEgdG9tbC10eXBlLXYxCi0tLQo=	legacy/parse-ok	
body-with-separator	LS0tCiEgbWQKLS0tCgpoZWxsbwo=	legacy/parse-ok	aGVsbG8K
body-without-separator	LS0tCiEgdG9tbC10eXBlLXYxCi0tLQpmaWxlLWV4dGVuc2lvbiA9ICdwbmcnCg==	legacy/parse-error-missing-separator	-

# New vectors for the format-only document model (Task 1.6).
# Scanned by TestDocumentRFCConformance in document_test.go.

invalid-prefix-X	LS0tCiEgbWQKWCBiYWQKLS0tCgo=	document/parse-error-invalid-prefix	-
malformed-no-space	LS0tCiFub3NwYWNlCi0tLQoK	document/parse-error-malformed-line	-
inline-body-with-at-ref	LS0tCkAgYmxha2UyYjI1Ni1hYmMKISBtZAotLS0KCmlubGluZQo=	document/parse-error-inline-body-with-at	-

# New vectors for RFC 0002 (content grammar). Content is still opaque at
# these envelope-level harnesses (RFC 0001 §Conformance) — none of these
# vectors exercise RFC 0002's content-grammar productions themselves, only
# that documents using RFC 0002's new content spellings remain RFC
# 0001-envelope-conforming, per RFC 0002's "existing decoders remain
# conforming" scope boundary. legacy/parse-ok's decoder
# (TypedMetadataCoderDefault) only wires `!` and `@`, so the unified-lock
# form on a `!` line is the only one that fits that outcome; the `-`/`<`
# forms need the full six-prefix decoder (document/parse-ok, added
# alongside these vectors).
#
# Plain-text view:
#   unified-lock-type              : "---\n! md @blake2b256-abc\n---\n"
#   unified-lock-reference         : "---\n! md\n- one/uno @blake2b256-def\n---\n"
#   field-predicate-line           : "---\n! task\n- due=\"2026-08-01\"\n---\n"
#   deprecated-angle-still-accepted: "---\n! task\n< blocks=other/task @blake2b256-ghi\n---\n"
#   id-less-field-lock             : "---\n! organize\n- _base=@blake2b256-jkl\n---\n"

unified-lock-type	LS0tCiEgbWQgQGJsYWtlMmIyNTYtYWJjCi0tLQo=	legacy/parse-ok	-
unified-lock-reference	LS0tCiEgbWQKLSBvbmUvdW5vIEBibGFrZTJiMjU2LWRlZgotLS0K	document/parse-ok	-
field-predicate-line	LS0tCiEgdGFzawotIGR1ZT0iMjAyNi0wOC0wMSIKLS0tCg==	document/parse-ok	-
deprecated-angle-still-accepted	LS0tCiEgdGFzawo8IGJsb2Nrcz1vdGhlci90YXNrIEBibGFrZTJiMjU2LWdoaQotLS0K	document/parse-ok	-
id-less-field-lock	LS0tCiEgb3JnYW5pemUKLSBfYmFzZT1AYmxha2UyYjI1Ni1qa2wKLS0tCg==	document/parse-ok	-

# RFC 0002 revised 2026-07-18: trailing line comments (`!`/`@`/`-`/`<`
# only, never `#` free text). This vector demonstrates the
# quoting-composes case the revision calls out explicitly: the `%`
# inside the quoted field value is content, not a comment boundary.
# Plain-text view:
#   trailing-comment-quoting-composes : "---\n! task\n- note=\"50% done\" % real comment\n---\n"

trailing-comment-quoting-composes	LS0tCiEgdGFzawotIG5vdGU9IjUwJSBkb25lIiAlIHJlYWwgY29tbWVudAotLS0K	document/parse-ok	-

# RFC 0002 revised 2026-07-20: the required space before a trailing
# comment's `%` is dropped (SP made optional, not required) — `%` is
# already self-delimiting (Reserved rune, excluded from every token
# charset), so a glued comment parses identically to a spaced one.
# Plain-text view:
#   trailing-comment-glued : "---\n! md%glued comment\n---\n"

trailing-comment-glued	LS0tCiEgbWQlZ2x1ZWQgY29tbWVudAotLS0K	legacy/parse-ok	-

# RFC 0003 (supersedes RFC 0002 §Lock grammar, 2026-07-18): a locked
# value is one atomic purpose-full markl id (no space before `@`),
# not a `SP @digest` suffix. unified-lock-type/unified-lock-reference/
# deprecated-angle-still-accepted above still decode fine (content is
# opaque at this envelope layer either way) but no longer demonstrate
# the canonical spelling — see RFC 0003 §Test Vectors.
# Plain-text view:
#   atomic-lock-type               : "---\n! md@blake2b256-abc\n---\n"
#   atomic-lock-reference           : "---\n! md\n- one/uno@blake2b256-def\n---\n"
#   atomic-lock-typed-edge           : "---\n! task\n- blocks=task/other@blake2b256-ghi\n---\n"
#   atomic-key-material-papi-shape  : "---\n! task\n- piggy-piv_auth-v1@ssh_ecdsa_nistp256_pub-qqxyz\n---\n"

atomic-lock-type	LS0tCiEgbWRAYmxha2UyYjI1Ni1hYmMKLS0tCg==	legacy/parse-ok	-
atomic-lock-reference	LS0tCiEgbWQKLSBvbmUvdW5vQGJsYWtlMmIyNTYtZGVmCi0tLQo=	document/parse-ok	-
atomic-lock-typed-edge	LS0tCiEgdGFzawotIGJsb2Nrcz10YXNrL290aGVyQGJsYWtlMmIyNTYtZ2hpCi0tLQo=	document/parse-ok	-
atomic-key-material-papi-shape	LS0tCiEgdGFzawotIHBpZ2d5LXBpdl9hdXRoLXYxQHNzaF9lY2RzYV9uaXN0cDI1Nl9wdWItcXF4eXoKLS0tCg==	document/parse-ok	-

# RFC 0003 revision (2026-07-18): MarklTerm's quoted-purpose form and
# the canonical spaced `=` field emission this revision standardizes.
# Plain-text view:
#   quoted-purpose-marklterm : "---\n! task\n- \"my thing\"@blake2b256-xyz\n---\n"
#   spaced-field-emission    : "---\n! task\n- due = \"2026-08-01\"\n---\n"

quoted-purpose-marklterm	LS0tCiEgdGFzawotICJteSB0aGluZyJAYmxha2UyYjI1Ni14eXoKLS0tCg==	document/parse-ok	-
spaced-field-emission	LS0tCiEgdGFzawotIGR1ZSA9ICIyMDI2LTA4LTAxIgotLS0K	document/parse-ok	-
